title: Chilled Korean Lentils Naengmyeon
description: A light, ovo‑vegetarian Korean noodle dish made from French lentils, served chilled with crisp vegetables and a tangy sesame‑soy dressing.
instructions: Rinse the lentils and soak them in water for 4 hours or overnight.
Drain the lentils and add them to a pot with fresh water, bring to a boil, then simmer until tender, about 20 minutes.
Mash the cooked lentils and mix in the lentil flour, salt, and water to form a smooth dough.
Knead the dough until elastic, then roll it thin using a rolling pin or pasta machine.
Cut the rolled dough into thin ribbons, then blanch in boiling water for 3 minutes.
Drain the noodles, rinse under cold water, and set aside to chill.
Slice cucumber and carrot into thin sticks and prepare boiled egg halves.
In a small bowl whisk together soy sauce, rice vinegar, sugar, and toasted sesame oil for the dressing.
Toss the chilled noodles with cucumber, carrot, eggs, and scallions, then drizzle the dressing over the top.
Serve immediately, garnished with extra scallions if desired.
